text.title: Google Ads API Authentication
text.summary: OAuth 2.0 + developer token authentication model, credential components, token access levels, and security rules.

list.item: Developer Token: API access identifier from ads.google.com
OAuth 2.0 Client ID: application identifier from Google Cloud Console
OAuth 2.0 Client Secret: application secret from Google Cloud Console
Refresh Token: long-lived token for generating access tokens
Login Customer ID: Manager Account (MCC) ID — required only for MCC access
Test Account: unlimited requests against test data — apply in Google Ads UI
Basic Access: 15,000 daily requests — requires approved application
Standard Access: 10,000 requests per customer — requires company verification

rule.statement: Use a Test Account developer token during development. Promote to Basic or Standard Access only when moving to production with real customer accounts.
rule.rationale: Test Account tokens are approved instantly; Basic and Standard Access require review and verification that blocks development if not planned ahead.

section.title: Authentication Flow

list.item: 2. Enable Google Ads API in the project
3. Create OAuth 2.0 credentials (web or desktop app)
4. Get Developer Token from ads.google.com account settings
5. Generate Refresh Token via OAuth flow
6. Configure client library with google-ads.yaml or dict

section.reference: google-ads.yaml template with all required credential fields
Python client initialization patterns using YAML and dict

rule.statement: Store all credentials in environment variables or a secrets manager. Never commit credentials to version control.
rule.rationale: Leaked credentials grant full API access to all linked ad accounts, enabling unauthorized spend.

list.item: Use separate credentials for test and production environments
Rotate refresh tokens periodically (at least annually)
Apply least-privilege access — only request scopes needed
